name: Dogoda
description: Dogoda is a minor deity in Slavic mythology, often described as the spirit or god of the west wind and gentle breezes. He is considered a benevolent figure, bringing pleasant weather and fostering growth. Dogoda is believed to be responsible for the light, refreshing winds that arrive after the harsh winter, helping to thaw the frozen earth and usher in the spring. He is associated with the warmth of the sun and the freshness of nature, symbolizing hope and renewal. Unlike some of the more powerful and dramatic Slavic deities, Dogoda is a subtle force, working quietly and steadily to bring about positive change. He is not typically depicted in grand myths or epic battles, but rather as a gentle presence felt in the rustling of leaves and the soft caress of the wind. Some accounts link him to the propagation of pollen, ensuring the fertility of the land and the abundance of crops. While direct worship of Dogoda might not have been widespread, his influence was likely acknowledged through reverence for nature and the elements, particularly the wind. His role highlights the Slavic appreciation for the delicate balance of the natural world and the importance of even the smallest forces in maintaining harmony. Dogoda's gentle nature stands in contrast to some of the more fearsome aspects of Slavic mythology, offering a comforting and optimistic perspective on the cycle of seasons and the power of nature.
subtitle: God of the West Wind and Breezes

keyMyths.title: Dogoda's Thaw
keyMyths.description: Dogoda's gentle breath melts the winter's ice, allowing spring to emerge. His warm winds carry the promise of new life to the frozen land.

keyMyths.title: The Pollen's Journey
keyMyths.description: Dogoda carries pollen on his breezes, ensuring the fertility of the fields and the abundance of crops. He is a silent guardian of the harvest.

keyMyths.title: Whispers of Hope
keyMyths.description: When people feel despair, Dogoda whispers words of encouragement on the wind. His message reminds them of the coming spring and the renewal of hope.

sources.author: Andrzej Szyjewski
sources.description: Overview of Slavic religion and deities.
sources.text: Overview of Slavic religion and deities.
